description:
You are a hydraulic engineer preparing a floodplain technical report for the White River at Muncie, IN. The baseline HEC-RAS unsteady simulation has been run with a 21,000 cfs peak flow (results in Muncie.p04.hdf).

Extract the peak Water Surface Elevation (WSE) and maximum channel velocity at every cross-section from the HDF5 results. Determine each cross-section's minimum channel bed elevation from the geometry data. Compute flood depth (peak WSE minus bed elevation) at each station.

Read ~/Documents/critical_infrastructure.csv (Facility_Name, River_Station, First_Floor_Elevation_ft). Linearly interpolate the peak WSE at each facility's river station from the cross-section profile and classify each as FLOODED (WSE > FFE) or SAFE.

Perform a flow sensitivity check: scale all flow values in the upstream boundary condition hydrograph in Muncie.b04 by 1.5x (multiply every flow value by 1.5, keeping times unchanged). Then re-run the simulation using the clean template (RasUnsteady Muncie.p04.tmp.hdf x04) and extract the resulting peak WSE at every cross-section. Compute the WSE increase (sensitivity minus baseline) per station.

Save results to:
- ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/floodplain_profile.csv: River_Station, Peak_WSE_ft, Max_Velocity_fps, Bed_Elev_ft, Flood_Depth_ft, Sensitivity_Delta_WSE_ft
- ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/infrastructure_impact.csv: Facility_Name, River_Station, FFE_ft, Interpolated_WSE_ft, Flood_Depth_ft, Status
- ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/report_summary.txt with labeled lines: NUM_CROSS_SECTIONS, MAX_FLOOD_DEPTH_FT, MAX_FLOOD_DEPTH_STATION, FACILITIES_FLOODED, FACILITIES_SAFE, MAX_SENSITIVITY_DELTA_FT
